item.title: Disease phenotype affects treatment of late-onset inflammatory bowel disease: Analysis of a large UK cohort.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in older adults is rising. Studies of late-onset (LO) (diagnosis ≥60 years of age) IBD are contradictory, with some suggesting increased use of co...

item.title: A gut vascular barrier damage-based scoring system predicts postoperative outcomes in Crohn's disease.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - The gut vascular barrier (GVB) is essential for maintaining intestinal homeostasis. However, its role in patients undergoing surgery for Crohn's disease (CD) remains unclear. Thus, our study aimed ...

item.title: Antibiotic Utilization for Perianal Fistulizing Complications Among Young Patients with Crohn's Disease.
item.description: The Journal of pediatrics - To characterize patterns of antibiotic use among children with Crohn's disease (CD) and perianal fistulizing complications (PFCs) via a large, national administrative database. Retrospective analys...

item.title: Εsophageal Crohn's disease in adults: A systematic review of the literature.
item.description: Surgery - Review - Esophageal involvement in Crohn's disease is rare and poorly understood, presenting with nonspecific symptoms that contribute to delayed diagnosis and inconsistent management. This systematic revie...

item.title: Appropriate J-pouch volume associated with improved clinical outcomes and long-term quality of life in patients with ulcerative colitis after ileal pouch-anal anastomosis: results from China UC Pouch Center Union.
item.description: Intestinal research - A well-functioning and well-constructed pouch is crucial for the long-term quality of life (QOL) in ulcerative colitis (UC) patients after ileal pouch-anal anastomosis (IPAA). What pouch volume is ...

item.title: Mesenteric preservation vs extended mesenteric excision in Crohn's disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: The surgeon : journal of the Royal Colleges of Surgeons of Edinburgh and Ireland - Review - There remains ongoing debate and lack of consensus regarding the benefit of extended mesenteric excision (EME) compared to limited mesenteric excision (LME) in the surgical management of Crohn's di...

item.title: Transcriptional and ubiquitinative suppression of macrophage CST3 disrupts colonic homeostasis through defective efferocytosis.
item.description: Cell death and differentiation - To elucidate the role and regulatory mechanisms of macrophage-derived cystatin C (CST3) in Crohn's disease (CD), focusing on colonic inflammation, macrophage-epithelial interactions, and barrier dy...

item.title: The Impact of Metabolic Bariatric Surgery on Inflammatory Bowel Disease Risk and Outcomes in Adults With Obesity: a Propensity-matched, Nationwide, Analysis.
item.description: Obesity surgery - The risk of de novo inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) after metabolic bariatric surgery (MBS) has been described, but the timing and severity of de novo IBD is unclear. Using MarketScan Databases, p...

item.title: Pattern-based histologic approach in colitis without chronic architectural damage: GIPAD recommendations.
item.description: Digestive and liver disease : official journal of the Italian Society of Gastroenterology and the Italian Association for the Study of the Liver - Review - In patients presenting with intestinal symptoms who undergo colonoscopy with mucosal sampling, the pathologist plays a central role in identifying the underlying etiology in order to guide appropri...

item.title: Venous Thromboembolism After Total Colectomy in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Risk Factors and Mortality - a Danish Population-Based Cohort Study.
item.description: Clinical epidemiology - Total colectomy is a key intervention in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management and a high-risk procedure for postoperative venous thromboembolism (VTE). We investigated risk factors for posto...

item.title: Bayesian prior elicitation on the efficacy of medical therapies in perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Robust evidence for most licensed Crohn's disease therapies is lacking for perianal fistula outcomes due to a lack of dedicated clinical trials. This study aimed to use a Bayesian framework to dete...
